mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment surge, invalidating the recession-fear transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to predict positive quarterly returns with a Rank IC above 0.05 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates robust manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, with out-of-sample directional accuracy consistently below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above 2.5 percent annually triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 65% of months where CPI exceeds the 2.5 percent threshold, breaking the inflation-to-rate-hike transmission mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that supports corporate earnings, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined payroll and unemployment signal shows no predictive power for 20-day returns, with a t-statistic below 1.5 over any 5-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding economic output and corporate revenue potential, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth rates fail to rank-correlate with quarterly equity returns, producing a Rank IC consistently below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the index of industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent signals contracting manufacturing activity and weakening supply-side momentum, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances following a 0.5 percent monthly drop in industrial production, refuting the supply-contraction risk premium.
